name: Layered Tamale with Green Salsa
recipeIngredient: 2 cups masa harina or finely ground yellow cornmeal
4 ounces chopped mild green chilies, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Shuck corn and save the husks; set aside.
2. Place husks in a large saucepan, cover with water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes. Drain in a colander and cool.
3. Generously oil a 1-1/2 quart souffle dish. Arrange husks in an overlapping spoke design to completely line the bottom and sides of the dish, extending about 2 inches past the rim. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside.
4. Cut corn from cobs and reserve.
5. In a large bowl, beat together masa, water, shortening, butter, salt, and baking powder to make a batter. Beat batter on medium speed for 5 minutes; fold in cheese. Remove 1/3 of the batter to a separate bowl with the corn and stir to combine. Spoon corn mix into prepared dish and smooth to level.
6. Place half of the remaining batter into a food processor bowl fitted with a chopping blade; add chilies, parsley, cilantro, and green onion. Mix until smooth. Spoon this mixture on top of the corn (second layer).
7. Rinse the processor bowl. In the processor with a chopping blade, use the rest of the batter, red pepper, chili powder, and hot sauce. Process until smooth. Spoon this mix on top.
8. Fold in the husks. To keep the husks down, weight the top of the tamale with an aluminum foil square filled with 1/2 cup raw rice or beans. Fill an 8-quart saucepan with 1-1/2 inches of water and insert a wire rack. Place the dish on the rack. Bring to boil over high heat; reduce to low, cover tightly, and steam for 1 hour.
9. Remove from pot (it will expand during cooking and shrink on cooling). Let cool for 1 hour. Remove foil. Invert the plate over and unmold. Invert again to get it right side up. Peel back husks. Cut into wedges.
Green Salsa:
- 4 ounces chopped mild green chilies, drained
- 1/4 cup fresh parsley
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro
- 1/4 cup green onion, chopped
- 1 medium sweet red pepper, chopped
- 1/2 teaspoon chili powder
- 1/4 teaspoon ground cumin
- 4 to 5 drops hot pepper sauce
